description_long: This object is a classic flip-style mobile phone with a metallic silver body and a distinct hinge mechanism. The front features a small square display window set within a black frame, likely for notifications or caller ID, and a subtle circular emblem above it. The back side reveals a larger main screen and a tactile keypad with raised, wavy buttons, typical of early 2000s mobile phones. The side views highlight the slim profile and the separation between the upper and lower halves, connected by a central hinge. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on durability and compactness, and the materials appear to be primarily plastic with metallic accents.
description: A classic silver flip phone with a small front display, a main screen, and a tactile keypad, featuring a robust hinge and compact design.
description_view_0: Front view shows a small square display window within a black frame and a circular emblem above it.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the slim profile and the hinge connecting the two halves.
description_view_2: Back view reveals the main screen and a raised, wavy keypad layout below it.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, emphasizing the thinness and hinge structure.
attribute_brand_logo: A small circular emblem is present above the front display window, but no specific brand or text is visible.
attribute_screen_display_content: Both the front and main screens appear blank with no visible content, icons, or interface elements.
attribute_camera_placement: No visible camera modules or lenses are present on any side of the object.
attribute_button_layout: A tactile keypad with multiple raised buttons is located below the main screen; no side or top buttons are visible.
